title: Calyptrochilum bokoyense
taxonomy: Calyptrochilum bokoyense is a genus of orchids, and it belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. The species, Calyptrochilum bokoyense, is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The classification of this plant includes its order, which is Asparagales, and its family, which is Orchidaceae. Furthermore, the classification also includes its subfamily, which is Epidendroideae.

intro: Calyptrochilum bokoyense, also known as the Bokoi orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Calyptrochilum bokoyense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
